growth_records.cite: Wang C et al. Epsilon-poly-L-lysine: Recent Advances in Biomanufacturing and Applications. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2021.748976. [PMC8506220]
growth_records.snippet: Streptomyces noursei NRRL 5126, fed batch fermentation 144 h, glucose + yeast extract, DO control (40% growth / 20% production) -> 2 g/L (Bankar and Singhal (2011))
growth_records.notes: Value CITED in this review (Wang et al. 2021, Front Bioeng Biotechnol) FROM primary paper Bankar and Singhal (2011); not measured in the review. Volumetric productivity derived = 2 g/L / 144 h = 0.0139 g/L/h. Medium given only qualitatively (glucose + yeast extract), not quantified -> medium_composition_stated:false. No growth rate, biomass, or substrate uptake reported here; epsilon-PL is the excreted product.
